text:
You borrowed $1,000 for your father's funeral. The kiln owner was glad to help.

Now you make his bricks until the debt is paid.

Lodging fee: +$150.

You didn't think the roof was free?
Your wife and son arrive at the kiln gates. They've come to help you pay it off.
The market is down, says the owner. Bricks pay $7 now.
Your shovel splits at the handle. A replacement is charged to the ledger: +$75.

Kiln fuel charge: +$120.

The fire doesn't feed itself.
The market is down again. Bricks pay $5 now.

Your wife is burned reaching into the kiln. Hospital bill: +$200.

She works slower now.
The owner recalculates your debt. Interest. Fees. It's all in the ledger: +$250.

The mold takes your son's hand. Hospital bill: +$400.

He works slower now.
Charges for food, lodging, and upkeep: +$${amount}.
